description:
To properly set the owner of /etc/ipsec.d, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /etc/ipsec.d
rationale:
The ownership of the /etc/ipsec.d directory by the root user is important
because this directory hosts Libreswan configuration. Protection of this
file is critical for system security. Assigning the ownership to root
ensures exclusive control of the Libreswan configuration.
ocil_clause: /etc/ipsec.d does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /etc/ipsec.d, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /etc/ipsec.d
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root
fixtext:
Change the owner of the file /etc/ipsec.d to root by running the following command:
$ sudo chown root /etc/ipsec.d
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/ipsec.d file must be owned by root.
title: Verify User Who Owns /etc/ipsec.d Directory
